title: EIA Electricity Monthly Update - Data Methodology
content: The U.S. Energy Information Administration defines the Electric Power Sector as comprising electricity-only and combined heat and power plants within the North American Industrial Classification System 22 category whose primary business is to sell electricity, or electricity and heat, to the public. In EIA's reporting, the All Sectors totals include the Electric Power Sector and the Commercial and Industrial Sectors, where Commercial and Industrial power producers are primarily combined-heat-and-power plants. Data published in the Electricity Monthly Update are compiled from two main survey forms: Form EIA-826, the Monthly Electric Utility Sales and Revenues with State Distributions Report, and Form EIA-923, the Power Plant Operations Report. The survey data are collected monthly using multiple-attribute cutoff sampling of power plants and electric retailers for the purpose of estimation for various data elements, such as generation, stocks, and revenue, across various categories. Regression-based, or prediction, methodologies are used to estimate totals from the sample. Essentially complete samples are collected for the Electric Power Monthly, which publishes state-level values, while the Electricity Monthly Update is based on an incomplete sample and includes only regional estimates and ranges for state values where applicable. EIA documents this methodology so that users of the electric-power series can see that published U.S. electric-power generation reflects a defined set of industry-22 plants, a defined set of survey forms, and a statistical estimation procedure designed for monthly frequency rather than a census. Analysts reading the industrial-production index for electric and gas utilities can match changes in that index against the EIA framework, since EIA's electric power data are the underlying authoritative count of U.S. electric generation and capacity.
